seo.title: GDPR Compliance implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping GDPR Compliance projects safely.

content.intro: This production-readiness checklist covers the technical and operational requirements for SaaS applications handling EU citizen data under GDPR. It focuses on engineering implementation, data lifecycle management, and infrastructure configuration to ensure compliance before going live.

items.title: Enforce Opt-In for Non-Essential Cookies
items.description: Block all non-essential scripts (marketing, tracking, non-anonymized analytics) from loading until the user explicitly clicks 'Accept' on the consent banner.

items.title: Implement 'Reject All' Parity
items.description: Ensure the 'Reject All' button is visually identical in prominence, size, and color to the 'Accept All' button to avoid dark patterns.

items.title: Log Consent State and Version
items.description: Store a timestamped record of the user's consent choice and the specific version of the privacy policy they agreed to in a tamper-evident log.

items.title: Verify Privacy-First Analytics Configuration
items.description: If using Plausible or Fathom, verify that IP hashing is enabled and that no persistent cookies are being dropped on the client side.

items.title: Granular Consent Controls
items.description: Provide users with a toggle menu to enable or disable specific categories of data processing (e.g., functional vs. marketing) independently.

items.title: Automated Deletion Workflow
items.description: Create a script or background job that identifies and deletes all database records associated with a user_id across all tables, including metadata.

items.title: Data Portability Export Tool
items.description: Provide a machine-readable export (JSON or CSV) of all personal data stored about a user, accessible via their account settings.

items.title: Backup Retention Alignment
items.description: Ensure that data deleted from production is also purged from database backups within 30 days, or implement a process to skip restoring deleted IDs.

items.title: Third-Party Deletion Propagation
items.description: Configure webhooks to trigger data deletion in integrated third-party tools (e.g., Intercom, Stripe, Postmark) when a user deletes their account.

items.title: Manual Verification Procedure
items.description: Document a standard operating procedure for verifying the identity of a user making a manual data request via email before releasing data.

items.title: Set EU Data Residency
items.description: Configure cloud provider regions to store primary databases and object storage (S3) within the European Economic Area (e.g., eu-central-1).

items.title: Enable Encryption at Rest
items.description: Verify that AES-256 encryption is enabled for all database volumes, backups, and cloud storage buckets.

items.title: TLS 1.3 Enforcement
items.description: Disable legacy SSL/TLS versions; configure load balancers to only accept TLS 1.2 or 1.3 for data in transit.

items.title: Staging Data Anonymization
items.description: Implement a pipeline to scrub or mask PII (emails, names, IPs) when syncing production data to staging or development environments.

items.title: Network Isolation
items.description: Deploy databases in private subnets with no public IP addresses, accessible only via a VPN or an internal application gateway.

items.title: PII Scrubbing in Logs
items.description: Configure application loggers to redact email addresses, passwords, and authorization headers before they are sent to external logging services.

items.title: Log Retention Policy
items.description: Set a TTL (Time To Live) on application and access logs to automatically delete data older than 30-90 days.

items.title: IP Address Anonymization
items.description: Truncate or hash the last octet of user IP addresses in server access logs unless specifically required for security forensics.

items.title: Admin Access Audit Logs
items.description: Maintain a non-mutable log of every instance where an internal employee accesses a user's personal data via an admin panel.

items.title: Breach Notification Alerting
items.description: Configure automated alerts for unusual spikes in data egress or multiple failed login attempts to detect potential breaches early.

items.title: Inventory of Sub-processors
items.description: Maintain a public-facing list of all third-party services that process user data, including their location and purpose.

items.title: DPA Execution
items.description: Verify that a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is signed and on file for every third-party service in the stack (e.g., AWS, Supabase, Postmark).

items.title: SCC Verification for US Vendors
items.description: For US-based vendors, ensure Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) are included in the DPA to address Schrems II requirements.

items.title: SDK Privacy Audit
items.description: Audit all client-side SDKs to ensure they do not automatically collect device identifiers or location data without explicit user consent.

items.title: Email Marketing Opt-out Sync
items.description: Ensure that 'Unsubscribe' actions in marketing emails immediately sync to the user's status in the primary application database.
